blockedAssertions.blockedBy: The shared app already had a seeded company, and in-browser zero-company mock injection could render `/onboarding` directly but did not hold through `/` route recovery; the app snapped back to the real `BOA` dashboard.

The same seeded-company limitation prevented auditable companyless recovery checks for `/dashboard`, `/issues`, and `/companies`; deep-link attempts reverted to real `BOA` routes before a stable zero-company redirect could be captured.

Prefixed onboarding could be reopened coherently, but I could not keep the zero-company global onboarding mock stable through refresh/back/forward without the session reverting to the seeded shared app state, so the full canonical-history matrix was not auditable.

The shared app was running in `local_trusted` mode, and auditable bootstrap-pending route blocking could not be produced because agent-browser request interception did not intercept local `/api/health` calls in this session.
I could not produce a stable authenticated signed-out redirect flow because local `/api/auth/get-session` and `/api/health` interception failed in the agent-browser session.
Bootstrap/auth blocking states could not be held stably enough to capture singular gate screens without falling back to the real local-trusted app state.
Because bootstrap/auth mock states were not stable on the shared app, I could not audibly verify retry/recovery transitions without losing the intended mocked route state.

The shared app did not enter bootstrap-pending mode during the session, and request interception/runtime overrides were insufficient to prove gate precedence over unresolved/signed-out auth on the real surface.

I could render the direct zero-company `/onboarding` shell, but I could not keep the shared seeded app in a stable zero-company state across `/`, `/dashboard`, and authenticated post-sign-in entry variants.

appliedUpdates.description: Added Browser UI flow-validator guidance for onboarding-entry bundles on the shared app.

Documented that the current shared onboarding mission app is seeded and local /api interception instability should block first-run root/deep-link and bootstrap/auth assertions rather than mutating shared state.

notes: Console errors observed via `agent-browser errors`: none.
Mock setup used only browser-local runtime overrides inside the assigned browser session; no product code or shared app processes were modified.
Shared app health at start: `{status: ok, deploymentMode: local_trusted, bootstrapStatus: ready}`.
Entity counts for VAL-ONBOARD-CROSS-002 were read via the shared local API only to prove zero mutation: before and after counts matched exactly.
